prompt: Why does speculative decoding speed up batch-1 CPU inference? One sentence.
output_a: Because a small draft model proposes several tokens that the large model verifies in a single forward pass, so the large weights are streamed from memory once per several tokens instead of once per token.
output_b: Because it makes the computer think faster and uses the GPU cores more efficiently to predict the future tokens ahead of time in parallel threads.
note: A is mechanistically correct (bandwidth amortization, no GPU assumed). B is hand-wavy and wrong for the CPU/batch-1 setting. Used to detect judge drift.
